name: 25_Speech_Emotion_Recognition_CNN_LSTM_RAVDESS_DL
query: Estoy buscando un proyecto de reconocimiento de emociones en el habla utilizando un modelo CNN-LSTM con el conjunto de datos RAVDESS, que debe descargarse de Kaggle o [este enlace de Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/xbgoose/ravdess). El proyecto debe cargar el conjunto de datos y realizar un preprocesamiento de audio robusto (eliminación de ruido y normalización) y extracción de características MFCC, implementado en `src/data_loader.py`. El modelo CNN-LSTM debe implementarse en `src/model.py`. La precisión del reconocimiento debe guardarse en `results/metrics/recognition_accuracy.txt`, y se debe generar una matriz de confusión y guardarse como `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`. Además, se debe crear una API local fácil de usar con Flask para permitir a los usuarios subir archivos de audio y recibir resultados de reconocimiento de emociones, con la implementación incluida en `src/hci.py`.

requirements.criteria: El conjunto de datos "RAVDESS" se carga en `src/data_loader.py`, que se descarga de Kaggle o [este enlace de Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/xbgoose/ravdess).
El preprocesamiento de audio, incluida la eliminación de ruido y la normalización, se implementa en `src/data_loader.py`.
La extracción de características MFCC se implementa en `src/data_loader.py`.
La precisión del reconocimiento se guarda en `results/metrics/recognition_accuracy.txt`.
La matriz de confusión se genera y guarda como `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.
Se crea una API local usando "Flask" para permitir a los usuarios subir archivos de audio y recibir resultados de reconocimiento de emociones. La implementación debe incluirse en `src/hci.py`.

preferences.criteria: El paso de preprocesamiento de audio debe ser robusto, reduciendo efectivamente el ruido mientras se preserva la integridad de las señales de habla.
La API local debe ser fácil de usar, con instrucciones claras para subir archivos e interpretar resultados.

judge_stats.criteria: El conjunto de datos "RAVDESS" se carga en `src/data_loader.py`, que se descarga de Kaggle o [este enlace de Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/xbgoose/ravdess).

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement specifies that the "RAVDESS" dataset should be loaded in `src/data_loader.py` from Kaggle or the provided Hugging Face link. While the trajectory evidence indicates that the dataset was successfully loaded from the Hugging Face link, the content of `src/data_loader.py` does not explicitly show the implementation of loading the dataset from either source. The file contains a placeholder function `load_dataset()` that returns a minimal dictionary without actual dataset loading logic.

judge_stats.criteria: El preprocesamiento de audio, incluida la eliminación de ruido y la normalización, se implementa en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The provided content of `src/data_loader.py` does not explicitly show the implementation of audio preprocessing, including noise removal and normalization. Although the trajectory evidence indicates that these functions were successfully executed after resolving a dependency issue, the actual code implementing these preprocessing steps is not visible in the provided file content. Therefore, without direct evidence of the implementation in the code, the criterion is not fully satisfied.

judge_stats.criteria: La extracción de características MFCC se implementa en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The provided content of `src/data_loader.py` does not explicitly show the implementation of MFCC feature extraction. The file contains placeholders and minimal semantic implementations, but there is no evidence of actual MFCC extraction code. Although the trajectory evidence mentions successful MFCC extraction after resolving an `ImportError`, the specific implementation details are not present in the provided code snippet.

<UNSATISFIED>: The requirement that the "CNN-LSTM" model is implemented in `src/model.py` is not satisfied. The content of `src/model.py` does not show any implementation of a CNN-LSTM model. Instead, it contains a placeholder function `build_model()` that returns a string "model placeholder" and does not include any actual model architecture or logic. Additionally, the trajectory evidence indicates an import error during execution, suggesting unresolved dependencies, further supporting the lack of a functional model implementation.

judge_stats.criteria: La precisión del reconocimiento se guarda en `results/metrics/recognition_accuracy.txt`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The content of `recognition_accuracy.txt` is shown as `('placeholder\r\n', None)`, which indicates that the actual recognition accuracy value is not stored in the file. Despite the trajectory evidence suggesting that the issue with directory permissions was resolved, the file content does not reflect the expected accuracy data.

judge_stats.criteria: La matriz de confusión se genera y guarda como `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: Although the trajectory evidence indicates that the confusion matrix was successfully generated and saved as `results/figures/confusion_matrix.png` after resolving the initial directory issue, the content of the file shows an error message indicating that the image file could not be read due to an unsupported image format. This suggests that the file may not be valid or correctly formatted, thus failing to meet the requirement of having a properly generated and saved confusion matrix.

judge_stats.criteria: Se crea una API local usando "Flask" para permitir a los usuarios subir archivos de audio y recibir resultados de reconocimiento de emociones. La implementación debe incluirse en `src/hci.py`.

reason: <UNSATISFIED>: The requirement specifies that a local API using "Flask" should be created to allow users to upload audio files and receive emotion recognition results, and this implementation should be included in `src/hci.py`. However, the content of `src/hci.py` provided does not show any implementation of a Flask API. It only contains a minimal semantic implementation with a function `main()` that returns a dictionary with status, notes, and keywords. There is no evidence of Flask being used to create an API for uploading audio files or processing emotion recognition. Additionally, the trajectory evidence indicates that while the API was eventually started, there were issues with exception handling for invalid audio files, suggesting incomplete functionality.
